original_title: Recent advances in the development of small-molecule drugs based on covalent reversible inhibitors
title: Reversible covalent inhibitors as a drug design strategy
blurb: A review of reversible covalent inhibitors for small-molecule drug design, where covalent binding can provide specificity and lower doses than noncovalent binders. The downside is synthetic complexity and residual risk of off-target covalent binding, so it works well in the right context but it's not a universal strategy.

original_title: Screening of Cell-Penetrating Peptides To Enhance the Therapeutic Efficacy of α-Helical Antimicrobial Peptides against Intracellular Bacterial Infections
title: Getting antimicrobial peptides into cells to fight intracellular bacteria
blurb: Cell-penetrating peptides as a strategy to ferry antimicrobial alpha-helical peptides into cells for intracellular bacterial infections. The idea is sound, but delivery efficiency and post-entry peptide activity are both serious obstacles that have held this approach back.

original_title: One-Pot Electrodeprotection and Horner–Wadsworth–Emmons Olefination of Acetals Using LiClO 4 –1,3,5-Trioxane Complex
title: Electrochemical acetal deprotection and HWE olefination in one pot
blurb: A one-pot procedure combining electrochemical acetal deprotection with Horner-Wadsworth-Emmons olefination using a LiClO4-1,3,5-trioxane complex. It's elegant in principle, but whether you'd actually use it instead of conventional protecting groups depends on substrate scope and real selectivity data.

original_title: Halogen-Bonding Complexes of Iodonium Ylides for Halogen Atom Transfer: C–H Alkylation of Heterocycles
title: C-H alkylation of heterocycles via halogen-bonded iodonium ylides
blurb: Using halogen-bonding complexes of iodonium ylides to alkylate C-H bonds on heterocycles through halogen atom transfer. The halogen bonding activation is elegant, but substrate scope and selectivity versus standard C-H methods will determine whether this actually competes in practice.

original_title: A lymph node-targeted cell-nanoadjuvant conjugate enhances dendritic cell-T cell crosstalk for cancer immunotherapy.
title: Lymph node-targeted adjuvant nanoparticles to prime T cells against cancer
blurb: A nanoparticle-adjuvant conjugate engineered to target lymph nodes and enhance dendritic cell activation for T cell priming in cancer immunotherapy. Concentrating immune response at the lymph node is sound strategy, but translating that into durable tumor control in vivo is what actually matters.

original_title: An Observational Study to Learn More About Treatment Patterns and Factors Determining the Choice of Treatment in Canadian Men With Metastatic Hormone Sensitive Prostate Cancer in Routine Medical Care
title: How Canadian doctors treat metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer in real practice
blurb: An observational study of real-world treatment patterns and decision factors for men with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer across Canadian clinical practice. Real-world data can fill gaps that trials miss, though separating what's driven by evidence from what's driven by access or inertia is the usual challenge.

original_title: ARX517/JNJ-95298177 as Monotherapy or Combination Therapy in Subjects With Metastatic Prostate Cancer
title: ARX517 plus apalutamide in metastatic prostate cancer
blurb: Phase 1 test pairing a radioligand with the standard AR inhibitor. Early work, underpowered for response rates, but useful for sorting out whether these two drugs synergize or just muddle each other.

original_title: Covalent reinforcement drives hierarchical multiscale structure-function integration in hybrid chicken-plant protein systems during extrusion-based 3D printing.
title: 3D-printed chicken-plant protein hybrids held together by covalent cross-links
blurb: Extrusion-based 3D printing of chicken and plant proteins with covalent reinforcement to lock in the structure. The strategy builds hierarchical organization across multiple scales, a plausible way to control the properties of printed food analogs.

original_title: Downstream invasive coronary procedures following PET Versus SPECT myocardial perfusion imaging: a propensity-matched analysis
title: PET myocardial perfusion imaging cuts angiography referrals versus SPECT
blurb: Propensity-matched retrospective analysis using TriNetX data to compare invasive coronary angiography rates within 90 days after PET versus SPECT myocardial imaging. If PET's higher specificity translates to fewer false positives driving workup, the data should show it, though selection bias despite matching remains a risk.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: Study the shielding capacity of some advanced epoxy ceramic composites against ionizing radiations
title: Epoxy composites filled with heavy metal oxide nanoparticles for radiation shielding
blurb: Epoxy loaded with 30-nm particles of zinc, copper, tungsten, and cerium oxides tested against gamma radiation from Am-241 and Cs-137. The nanoparticles improve shielding without the density of solid metal, though how much loading you can actually pack in without embrittling the matrix isn't clear.

original_title: A Unified Platform for Direct Alkynylation from Alcohols via Deoxygenative Metallaphotoredox Catalysis.
title: Deoxygenative alkynylation of alcohols in one metallaphotoredox step
blurb: A unified catalytic platform converting alcohols directly to alkynes via merged metal and photoredox catalysis, bypassing the usual protecting-group detours. The strategy is clean in principle, though actual utility depends on substrate scope and how selective the deoxygenation stays with mixed functional groups present.

original_title: Denuded peptidoglycan oligosaccharides enable the biochemical investigation of bacterial cell wall recognition, modification, and degradation
title: Synthetic peptidoglycan oligomers for studying bacterial cell wall enzymes
blurb: Chemically defined denuded peptidoglycan fragments as substrates for interrogating recognition and degradation machinery. The purity and homogeneity beat whole-cell chaos, though you lose the spatial organization and mechanical stress of the intact wall.

original_title: A Secular Growth Story Emerging in Radiopharmaceuticals - William Blair
title: Radiopharmaceuticals shaping up as a secular growth story
blurb: William Blair's investment thesis on radiopharmaceuticals as an emerging multi-decade growth market in biotech. The case hinges on expanding clinical adoption and supply constraints easing, though most programs are still early and competition from conventional therapies remains steep.

original_title: CD117 epitope-shielded hematopoietic stem cell transplantation with toxin-free conditioning and in vivo selection ameliorates β-thalassemia model
title: Shielding engineered HSPCs from myeloablative conditioning in thalassemia
blurb: Masking the CD117 epitope on genetically corrected hematopoietic stem cells so they survive conditioning that kills off the host immune system, allowing transplantation without the usual toxicity. The approach targets the main barrier to gene therapy in thalassemia, though it's been tested only in a model and translating to the clinic requires showing that epitope masking sticks and doesn't cripple engraftment.

original_title: Transport specificity of FpvA and FpvB for pyoverdine–antibiotic conjugates in Pseudomonas aeruginosa
title: FpvA and FpvB: the gates for antibiotic–pyoverdine conjugates in Pseudomonas
blurb: The two outer membrane transporters that import pyoverdine-antibiotic conjugates into Pseudomonas aeruginosa, offering either a target for evasion or an exploit for delivery. Mapping their substrate specificity matters for designing conjugates that work, though transporter loss is a straightforward resistance mechanism bacteria already deploy.

original_title: Multiple Brønsted acidic sites in nitrogen-rich dicyandiamide immobilized on silica gel as a heterogeneous catalyst for efficient conversion of fructose into 5-hydroxymethylfurfural
title: A reusable acidic heterogeneous catalyst for fructose-to-HMF conversion
blurb: A nitrogen-rich catalyst on silica converts fructose to HMF with 95.2% yield in DMSO and survives multiple cycles. The high conversions are real, though moving from DMSO to an industrially viable solvent is a step they haven't taken yet.

original_title: How chemists are using LEDs to create new synthetic methods - Science (AAAS)
title: LEDs enable two-carbon functionalization from a single C-X bond
blurb: Photochemistry generates a radical-cation intermediate from a single C-X bond, hitting both adjacent carbons without requiring an alkene or pre-functionalization at both positions. Mechanistically cleaner and experimentally simpler than the traditional routes. It's early-stage research, though, so substrate scope will tell you if this actually generalizes.

original_title: Development of a High-Throughput Size-Exclusion Chromatography–Mass Spectrometry Workflow for Real-Time Monitoring of Bioconjugation of Cysteine-Based Antibody–Drug Conjugates
title: Real-time ADC bioconjugation monitoring with SEC-MS
blurb: A size-exclusion chromatography-mass spectrometry workflow that watches cysteine-based ADC conjugation as it happens, giving you drug-to-antibody ratios without waiting for end-point analytics. High-throughput means you can screen linker and payload combinations faster. Whether that's actually where your bottleneck is—conjugation rather than formulation, manufacturing, or animal testing—is another question.

original_title: Intrinsic N-Terminal Reactivity and Improved Analysis of DSSO-Carbamate and Carbamate-Based Cross-Linkers
title: DSSO-carbamate extends the cross-linker shelf life
blurb: Replacing NHS ester groups with carbamates in DSSO keeps the cross-linker stable longer before it hydrolyzes, so you lose less reagent to side reactions. The tradeoff is slower reactivity at lysines, which you can compensate for with reaction time and concentration. Whether the stability gain outweighs the kinetic penalty depends on whether linker degradation was actually your problem.

original_title: Site-specific labelling of native peptides and proteins: chemical and enzymatic strategies
title: Site-specific protein labeling, and why it stays in the lab
blurb: A review of chemical and enzymatic strategies for site-specific protein labeling. The site-specificity part is achievable, but most methods require protein engineering, orthogonal chemistry, or custom enzymes that make them slower and more expensive than basic conjugation. That scalability problem is the real limitation for translational use.

original_title: A Bioorthogonally Cleavable N-Oxide Linker for Prodrug Design: Elucidating the Structure-Cyclization Kinetics Relationships for Controlled Drug Release.
title: An N-oxide linker that bioorthogonal chemistry can trigger
blurb: A linker design that stays intact in vivo until you trigger it with a bioorthogonal reaction to release the drug. The authors map how structure changes affect cyclization kinetics, giving you tuning parameters for release timing. It's a useful tool for prodrug design in theory, though bioorthogonal reactions in vivo are still finding their footing, and you'd need an activator that reaches the right place.

original_title: New measurements of the half-life of <sup>225</sup>Ac at the NPL and IRA
title: A new half-life measurement for Actinium-225
blurb: NPL and IRA remeasured Ac-225, the alpha emitter that radiotherapy keeps designing trials around while worrying about supply. Precise decay constants matter for dose calculations and inventory tracking during storage. If this improves precision or resolves prior measurement disagreements, it helps calibrate dosimetry calculations more confidently, though clinical use is still limited by Ac-225 availability overall.

original_title: A Case Study on the Impact of Thermal Hydrolysis and Lactone Ring Opening of Linker-Payload on Drug-to-Antibody Ratio Determination in a Topoisomerase I Inhibitor-Based ADC
title: Heat can open your ADC linker and throw off drug-to-antibody ratios
blurb: A topoisomerase I inhibitor ADC with a lactone linker showed different drug-to-antibody ratios when the sample warmed up, because the lactone ring opens and increases the apparent drug loading. It's a case study on one ADC, but the broader lesson is that your sample prep and storage temperature matter more than you'd think if your linker isn't totally stable.

original_title: Camptothecin-, dolastatin- and duocarmycin-derived antibody-drug conjugates and their journey toward the clinic
title: Three natural product classes for ADCs now entering trials
blurb: Camptothecin-, dolastatin-, and duocarmycin-based ADCs, each with different toxicity profiles and mechanisms of action. Targeting rescues payloads too toxic to give directly, though GI and marrow toxicity still shows up. Whether one payload class will prove superior in humans is what the clinical programs should answer.

original_title: An Amphibious Bifunctional Probe for Protein Chemical Cross-Linking
title: An amphibious cross-linker that maps hydrophobic protein regions
blurb: A bifunctional probe for chemical cross-linking mass spectrometry that bridges both hydrophobic and hydrophilic protein surfaces, so you're not stuck just labeling surface lysines. Better coverage for mapping conformational dynamics and protein-protein interfaces. The method is solid, but interpreting distance constraints from a cross-link is still indirect enough that you'd want orthogonal data.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: On-resin assembly of cysteine-reactive linkers for controlled site-selective antibody bioconjugation.
title: On-resin linker synthesis for cysteine-selective antibody conjugation
blurb: A solid-phase synthesis approach where you build your custom linker on resin, then use it to conjugate payload to engineered antibody cysteines. Gives you control over linker structure and site-selectivity without in-solution linker stability issues, so fewer by-products and more defined drug-to-antibody ratios. The upfront engineering cost limits it to programs with enough lead time to justify it.

original_title: Rapid and Efficient Antibody–Drug Conjugate Design Using Mechanistic Bottom-Up Modeling from In Vitro to Human
title: Mechanistic modeling to predict ADC behavior from bench to clinic
blurb: A bottom-up modeling framework that takes in vitro conjugation and PK data to predict ADC performance in humans, aiming to speed up the design loop before you commit to manufacturing. If it works, you'd catch problematic linker or payload choices early and not waste time on dead ends. The models are only as good as your input assumptions, and phase 1 data has a way of humbling predictions.

original_title: Clicking 1,4-DithiinConjugated Dimaleimides for ChiropticalEvolution and Nanofabrication
title: A Michael addition click for amine-maleimide reactions
blurb: Maleimide-thiol clicks are standard, but amine versions run slowly, so they built one via Michael addition using 1,4-dithiin-conjugated dimaleimides instead. Works for chiral synthesis and nanofabrication, though the snippet doesn't say whether the scope is broad or if you're still tethered to a few substrates.

original_title: Radiochemistry and comparative in vitro assessment of PSMA-617 labeled with lead-212 (212Pb), actinium-225 (225Ac), and lutetium-177 (177Lu)
title: Testing Pb-212 and Ac-225 against Lu-177 in PSMA-targeted therapy
blurb: Pluvicto (Lu-177 PSMA-617) treats metastatic prostate cancer but the cytotoxicity is modest, which is why people have looked at alpha emitters like Pb-212 and Ac-225. They labeled the same PSMA-targeting vector with all three and compared them in vitro, though in vivo efficacy is the real measure.

original_title: GMP-compliant automated synthesis and quality control of [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-I&T using the GAIA/LUNA Elysia-Raytest module for the treatment of metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer
title: Automated GMP synthesis of a Lu-177 PSMA radioligand
blurb: Manufacturing Lu-177 radiopharmaceuticals on demand usually means hands-on radiochemistry, which is a bottleneck. They developed an automated GMP-compliant synthesis for PSMA-I&T on a GAIA/LUNA module, though the full paper is behind the journal.

original_title: Methods for Inferring Interaction Potentials from Cross-Linking Mass Spectrometry Data
title: Inferring interaction potentials from cross-linking mass spec data
blurb: XL-MS data tell you which proteins are close but not their interaction energies, making it hard to build coarse-grained simulation models. They've worked out systematic methods for extracting interaction potentials from pairwise proximity measurements, though the validation section got cut off.

original_title: Development of an automated radiosynthesis for [68Ga]Ga-NODAGA[40Lys]-Exendin-4 in sodium acetate buffer
title: Automating Ga-68 NODAGA-Exendin-4 radiolabeling for GLP-1 imaging
blurb: Exendin-4 tagged with Ga-68 via NODAGA lets you image GLP-1 receptors, but the short half-life means synthesis has to be fast. They built an automated radiosynthesis in sodium acetate buffer, though details on yield and specific activity are behind the journal.

original_title: Antibody-Drug Conjugates in Solid Tumor Oncology and the Frontier of Precision Immunosuppression: A Mechanistic, Translational, and Clinical Review.
title: ADCs versus the solid tumor microenvironment, reviewed
blurb: Antibody-drug conjugates work in blood cancers but struggle in solid tumors, where immunosuppression dampens even targeted therapies. This mechanistic and clinical review examines that gap and clinical strategies to close it, including the precision immunosuppression angle.

original_title: Synthesis and Preliminary Evaluation of the (211)At-Labeled PARP Inhibitor [(211)At]Talazoparib as a Targeted Alpha-Particle Emitting Therapeutic.
title: At-211-labeled talazoparib for targeted alpha therapy of PARP-sensitive cancers
blurb: Talazoparib is a PARP inhibitor used systemically, but labeling it with the alpha emitter At-211 lets you concentrate the dose on tumor cells for dual-hit DNA damage plus synthetic lethality. They synthesized and did preliminary evaluation of [211At]talazoparib, a proof-of-concept if the in vivo efficacy holds.

original_title: Multiscale design of chemically crosslinked dextran hydrogels from bulk scaffolds to microgels and nanofibers
title: Design rules for epoxide-crosslinked dextran hydrogels across scales
blurb: Dextran hydrogels are modular but predicting structure from crosslinking chemistry is nontrivial, so they synthesized design rules. This review connects epoxide crosslinkers (epichlorohydrin, EGDE, BDDE) and processing to network formation, morphology, and performance from bulk scaffolds down to nanofibers.

original_title: Genetic Encoding of a Trifunctional Photo‐Cross‐Linker with a Cleavable Alkyl Ester Moiety
title: Genetically encoded photo-crosslinker amino acid with a cleavable ester
blurb: Photo-crosslinker amino acids freeze protein interactions in living cells, but cleavable ones that let you release the complex for analysis are rare. They engineered a trifunctional PAA with an alkyl ester trigger, expanding what you can capture and release without leaving the cell.

original_title: Scaling the production of bromine-76 and bromine-77 labeled poly-ADP-ribose-polymerase-targeted theranostics for preclinical use
title: Scaling Br-76/77-labeled PARP inhibitor for theranostic preclinical studies
blurb: Bromine-76 (positron emitter for PET) and Br-77 (Auger emitter for therapy) can both label the same PARP inhibitor, giving you image and therapy in parallel. They worked out how to scale production of [76/77Br]RD1 (a rucaparib-derived compound) for preclinical use, a neat idea if Auger-electron doses actually pack enough punch.

original_title: Development of NOTA-based antibody-fragment labeling agent that reduces early renal radioactivity levels
title: NOTA ligand that clears faster from the kidneys
blurb: A NOTA-based labeling agent designed to reduce early renal uptake compared to standard approaches. The goal is keeping radioactivity out of the kidneys for cleaner imaging. Worth knowing whether it actually beats what's already in clinical use.

original_title: Phage-Selected Clickable Gln-Donor Peptide for Lys-Selective Fab Labeling Using Engineered Microbial Transglutaminase
title: Using transglutaminase to label a specific lysine on antibodies
blurb: A phage-selected peptide and engineered transglutaminase do site-selective modification at a native lysine without noncanonical amino acids. You need both phage selection and enzyme engineering, so it trades one headache for another.

original_title: Quality and pratical determinants in [(99m)Tc]Tc-tetrofosmin preparation: hygrometry, syringe sorption & maximal activity per kit.
title: Humidity and syringe type both matter for Tc-99m tetrofosmin
blurb: Hygrometry and syringe material noticeably shift the yield and radiochemical purity of Tc-99m tetrofosmin kit preparations. The kind of practical detail labs usually find by trial and error.

original_title: Bioorthogonal chemistry-driven multi-enzyme strategy for efficient terpenoid biosynthesis.
title: Bioorthogonal scaffolding for a terpenoid biosynthesis pathway
blurb: Bioorthogonal chemistry spatially organizes a multi-enzyme terpenoid pathway for higher flux. Enzyme proximity helps when it works, but scaling synthetic pathways like this is still the hard part.

original_title: Bis-Tetrazine Fluorogenic (Silicon)-Rhodamine Dyes for Live-Cell Labeling.
title: Tetrazine-quenched silicon-rhodamines for live-cell imaging
blurb: Bis-tetrazine fluorogenic dyes based on silicon-rhodamine turn on after bioorthogonal reaction. The silicon core should give you better photostability and redder emission than regular rhodamines.

original_title: Quality and pratical determinants in [99mTc]Tc-tetrofosmin preparation: hygrometry, syringe sorption & maximal activity per kit
title: Tc-99m tetrofosmin yields depend on moisture and syringe choice
blurb: Air humidity and syringe material both affect the quality of Tc-99m tetrofosmin preparations, particularly radiochemical purity. The kind of troubleshooting detail worth standardizing in your own lab.

original_title: Thiol-Retaining N-Terminal Cysteine Chemistry for Dual Modification and Bicyclic Peptide Construction
title: N-terminal cysteine for protein labeling without noncanonical amino acids
blurb: The 1,2-aminothiol on an N-terminal cysteine is reactive enough for site-selective modification of native peptides and proteins. Simpler than engineering in click handles and works in vivo, though existing 1,2-aminothiol reagents have their own rough edges.

original_title: Practical guidelines for the AMI-Isonitrile ligation.
title: How to run the AMI-isonitrile ligation
blurb: A practical protocol for the AMI-isonitrile ligation. Methods papers like this are mostly useful to people actually at the bench trying it.

original_title: Done EAZY: An Automated Procedure for 89 Zr-Radiolabeling and Size-Exclusion Chromatography Purification of Nanoliposomal Anticancer Therapeutics
title: Automated Zr-89 labeling and purification for nanoliposomal drugs
blurb: An automated workflow that runs both Zr-89 radiolabeling and size-exclusion purification for nanoliposomal anticancer therapeutics. Useful for throughput if you're labeling multiple batches, though your specific nanoparticle formulation limits what conjugates you can handle.

original_title: Near-Infrared and Shortwave Infrared Building Blocks for Activity-Based Sensors in Animals
title: NIR and shortwave-infrared dyes for animal activity-based sensing
blurb: A toolkit of near-infrared and shortwave-infrared fluorophores for activity-based probes in living animals. The longer wavelengths give better tissue penetration and less autofluorescence than visible light, though you trade worse photostability.

original_title: Unfolding-assisted Bayesian quantification of radionuclide mixtures in NaI(Tl) spectra with overlapping gamma-ray peaks
title: Picking apart overlapping gamma-ray peaks
blurb: Unfolding method plus Bayesian stats for when gamma-ray peaks pile on top of each other in NaI(Tl) spectra. Real problem in radionuclide analysis, and the paper claims it works better than the usual deconvolution approaches. Worth checking if your peaks actually look like their test cases.

original_title: Protein-protein interactions in Escherichia coli: Using cross-linking/mass spectrometry (XLMS) to reveal our current knowledge gaps in interactomes.
title: Mapping E. coli protein interactions with cross-linking MS
blurb: Cross-linking MS captures protein-protein interactions in E. coli, but the real insight here is what the method misses and what we don't understand about the interactome. It's a survey of current capabilities and blind spots, not a catalogue of new protein complexes.

original_title: Review: Phosphorus-33 – Analysis of nuclear reactor production methods for potential application in radionuclide therapy for bone metastases
title: Phosphorus-33 production routes for bone metastasis therapy
blurb: A review of reactor methods for producing P-33, a candidate for radionuclide therapy in bone metastases. It's a field survey, not a new production route, though the authors flag which methods look most scalable given current reactor capabilities.

original_title: Structure-Guided Pegylation of IL-1RA Preserves Fold and Antagonist Function
title: PEGylating IL-1RA without breaking it
blurb: Pegylation of interleukin-1 receptor antagonist guided by structural modeling to preserve the fold and drug activity. PEG often disrupts protein function, so structure-guided placement is how you work around it, and in this case the function held up, though that's protein-dependent.

original_title: Niowave, Inc. and Ratio Therapeutics Sign Supply Agreement for Actinium-225 to Advance Targeted Alpha Therapies - BioSpace
title: Niowave and Ratio team up on Actinium-225 supply
blurb: Niowave and Ratio Therapeutics sign a supply agreement for Ac-225, the isotope everyone wants for targeted alpha therapy but nobody can make enough of. If Niowave actually scales production, this unclogs a real pipeline bottleneck, though supply deals often sound better on paper than they perform in practice.

original_title: The equivalent volume method for gamma spectrometry calibration: A new method bridging the gap between distributed and point sources
title: A gamma calibration method for point and distributed sources
blurb: A new method (equivalent volume) that handles both point sources and distributed sources in gamma spectrometry, sidestepping the usual conversion between them. The paper claims it's simpler than picking one model and running corrections, though whether your actual setup needs it depends on your source geometry.

original_title: p ‐Benzoyl‐ l ‐phenylalanine as a Multifunctional Noncanonical Amino Acid in Synthetic Biology: Photoprobing, Photocatalysis, and Structural Programming for Biocontainment
title: p-Benzoyl-l-phenylalanine for photochemistry in synthetic biology
blurb: pBzF is a noncanonical amino acid with a benzophenone side chain that undergoes near-quantitative intersystem crossing to a reactive triplet state, giving you a site-specific photoreactive handle for photocrosslinking and photocatalysis. The bulky side chain also contributes steric effects useful for structural programming, and people have tried it for biocontainment, though that's more specialized.

original_title: Antibody-drug conjugates in selected solid tumours: a position statement update based on findings from the third workshop held by the ETOP IBCSG Partners Foundation.
title: Where ADCs stand in solid tumors: what ETOP IBCSG say
blurb: An update on antibody-drug conjugates in solid tumors, based on ETOP IBCSG's third workshop on the topic. ADCs have clearer wins in blood cancers, so this is the experts' take on solid tumors specifically and what the clinical data supports.

original_title: Optimizing [(99m)Tc]Tc-Tamsulosin for Enhanced Tumor Detection: A Comprehensive Approach from Preparation to In Vivo Evaluation.
title: Technetium-99m Tamsulosin as a tumor imaging tracer
blurb: Optimization of [(99m)Tc]Tc-Tamsulosin from synthesis through in vivo evaluation for tumor detection. Tamsulosin targets alpha-1 receptors that some tumors express, so the bet is that hot-labeling it gives you an imaging handle, though whether it beats existing tracers on specificity or background is something to check against the competition.

original_title: Oligonucleotides Post‐Synthetic Modifications: An Overview of Clickable Nucleoside Phosphoramidites Suitable for Solid‐Phase Synthesis
title: Clickable nucleotides for attaching functional groups after synthesis
blurb: An overview of clickable nucleoside phosphoramidites for oligonucleotide modification by bioorthogonal click chemistry, which lets you attach functional cargo after solid-phase synthesis instead of during. It's faster and more modular than in-line modification, and click is robust enough that you can conjugate pretty much anything biocompatible at the end.

original_title: Adaptive Zinc-Amide Catalysis Enables C(sp 2 )–O Cross-Coupling of Aryl Fluorides with Alcohols
title: Aryl ethers from aryl fluorides and alcohols
blurb: A ligand-centered, redox-controlled zinc-amide system that couples aryl fluorides with alcohols into aryl alkyl ethers. Selective and designed around inexpensive feedstocks, though practical applicability hinges on substrate scope.

original_title: Systematic Investigation of Fatty Acids with Different Carbon-Chain Length as Albumin Binder to Improve Tumor Accumulation of Radioligands
title: Tuning fatty-acid chain length for albumin-radioligand binding
blurb: Varying the carbon-chain length of fatty acids to optimize their interaction with albumin, aiming to boost tumor uptake of radioligands. The systematic approach suggests clear winners emerged, but the abstract doesn't reveal which chain length actually won.

original_title: Computationally guided cross-linking overcomes interfacial mismatch in protein dimerization: Creating a long-acting cocaine esterase.
title: Designed cross-links stabilize cocaine esterase dimers
blurb: Computationally guided cross-linking to stabilize the mismatched interface of a cocaine esterase dimer. The goal is a longer-acting enzyme, though whether the cross-links preserve catalytic activity is the practical question.

original_title: Photoinduced Copper-Catalyzed Site- and Stereoselective Desymmetrizing β-C(sp 3 )-H Alkynylation of Cycloalkyl Amines
title: Site- and stereo-selective alkynylation of cycloalkyl amines
blurb: Photoinduced copper catalysis that inserts an alkyne at the β-C(sp³)–H of cycloalkyl amines while breaking symmetry and controlling stereochemistry. Selective C–H insertion is usually the bottleneck, so if it's clean, it's useful.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: On‐Demand Chemically Degradable Hydrogels for Biological Applications
title: Hydrogels that dissolve on chemical command
blurb: Crosslinked hydrogels designed to degrade when exposed to a small-molecule trigger. Applications include wound care, implant interfaces, drug depots, and cell culture, though the kinetics and selectivity of the trigger matter more than the concept.

original_title: The gastrin-releasing peptide receptor in oncology: a comprehensive review of recent advances in targeted radionuclide theranostics.
title: GRPR-targeted radionuclide theranostics in cancer
blurb: A review of gastrin-releasing peptide receptor as a target for both imaging and radiotherapy using radionuclides. GRPR is a logical target given its expression on tumors, though whether the clinical reality matches the preclinical promise is a question these reviews usually gloss over.

original_title: Antibody–drug conjugates in non-small cell lung cancer: current landscape and future directions
title: Antibody-drug conjugates move into lung cancer
blurb: ADCs targeting TROP2, HER2, and c-MET are gaining traction in non-small cell lung cancer, offering an alternative to small-molecule targeted therapy. Recent trials show benefit, but whether durability and selectivity hold up remains the open question.

original_title: Design and development of a semi-automated solid target assembly for the indigenous production of medical radioisotopes in the PETrace-800 cyclotron
title: Automated target assembly for the PETrace-800 cyclotron
blurb: Semi-automated solid target assembly to speed up radioisotope production on a PETrace-800 cyclotron for domestic supply. Automation usually means fewer errors and faster throughput, though whether it actually reduces per-isotope cost remains to be seen.

original_title: Theranostic strategies in prostate cancer: Advances in PSMA PET imaging and radioligand therapy.
title: PSMA theranostics for prostate cancer
blurb: An overview of PSMA-targeted PET imaging paired with PSMA-directed radioligand therapy for prostate cancer. Imaging to confirm expression and then treating with a matched radionuclide is proven, but response durability and patient selection remain the open problems.

original_title: PSMAddition Trial of ADT, ARPI, and Lutetium-177 in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer - Fred Saad - UroToday
title: Lu-177, ARPI, and ADT in hormone-sensitive prostate cancer
blurb: The PSMAddition trial tests combining a PSMA-targeted beta-emitter, an androgen receptor inhibitor, and standard ADT in metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer. Three modalities at once is the pitch, though whether it actually improves survival or just toxicity requires watching the full readout.

original_title: Experimental study of [(68)Ga]Ga-HER2-RGD, a dual-target molecular probe, for breast cancer PET/CT imaging.
title: A Ga-68 PET probe with dual HER2-RGD targeting for breast cancer
blurb: PET imaging probe labeled with Gallium-68, targeting both HER2 and the RGD peptide domain in breast cancer tissue. Early experimental work; whether the dual-targeting improves specificity or detection over simpler single-target approaches needs demonstration.

original_title: Polysaccharide–Peptide Conjugates as Precision Biomaterials: Conjugation Chemistry, Structural Design, and Biomedical Applications
title: Polysaccharide-peptide conjugates as modular biomaterials
blurb: Carbohydrate-peptide hybrids that let you tune peptide loading, position, and function independently from the scaffold. Covalent, bioorthogonal, and enzymatic coupling strategies control density, accessibility, and stability, with applications spanning molecular recognition, degradation, antimicrobial activity, and signaling. Mostly a design framework for now, but the modularity is useful when your application needs both a biological scaffold and peptide function.

original_title: Synthesis of optically active cyclopropanes by functionalization of cyclopropenes
title: Making chiral cyclopropanes from cyclopropenes
blurb: Enantioselective catalysis converts racemic cyclopropenes into optically active cyclopropanes, or diastereoselective reactions work on chiral starting material. Direct routes beat building the ring from scratch when you already have a three-membered ring in hand.

original_title: Comparative photochemical flow synthesis of ketenimines via Hg-lamp and UV-C LED irradiation
title: UV-C LEDs outperform mercury lamps for ketenimine synthesis
blurb: UV-C LEDs beat Hg-lamps for making ketenimines from isoxazoles in flow synthesis, suppressing secondary photoreactions that usually require high dilutions. Higher photon efficiency cuts residence times significantly, though UV-C LEDs are newer and not yet the standard in most shops.

original_title: Expanding 68Ga radiolabeling capacity: off-label use of PSMA-11 single vial cold kits through aliquoting and dual generator utilization
title: Squeezing more Ga-68 from PSMA-11 cold kits
blurb: Off-label reuse of single-vial PSMA-11 cold kits via aliquoting, plus dual generator operation to increase throughput without new equipment. Practical for centers that need higher capacity, though this lives in regulatory gray territory.

original_title: Zwitterionic and carboxyl-functionalized magnetic polystyrene beads for targeted biomolecular capture
title: Magnetic polystyrene beads with tunable capture chemistry
blurb: Porous polystyrene spheres with in-situ Fe₃O₄ loading for magnetism and dual-functionalized surfaces (zwitterionic and carboxyl) for selective binding. Silica coating improves stability and the surface chemistry is tunable for different biomarkers, though binding specificity to your actual target still needs validation.

original_title: Rhodium(III)-Catalyzed [3 + 2] Annulation of Diazo Compounds with Enaminones for Constructing Polysubstituted NH-Pyrroles
title: A Rh(III) annulation building pyrroles from diazo and enaminones
blurb: Rhodium(III)-catalyzed [3+2] coupling of diazo compounds and enaminones to make polysubstituted NH-pyrroles. Whether this works for the substrates you actually need depends on seeing the full scope, which the journal title doesn't cover.

original_title: Tisotumab Vedotin from Genmab - antibody-drug conjugate targets recurrent cervical cancer - ad-hoc-news.de
title: Genmab's tisotumab vedotin for recurrent cervical cancer
blurb: Tisotumab Vedotin is an antibody-drug conjugate targeting tissue factor for recurrent cervical cancer from Genmab. Beyond the headline, this is a clinical development program waiting for efficacy data.

original_title: Enhertu® Approved in the EU as First Tumor Agnostic HER2 Directed Therapy and Antibody Drug Conjugate for Patients with Previously Treated HER2 Positive Metastatic Solid Tumors - Yahoo! Finance Canada
title: Enhertu wins EU approval as a tumor-agnostic HER2 therapy
blurb: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (Enhertu) got EU approval for previously treated patients with HER2-positive solid tumors across multiple cancer types, a first for an ADC with a tumor-agnostic label. The efficacy data span multiple histologies, though whether these benefits hold across such diverse tumor types in routine practice is still an open question.

original_title: Visible Light‐Driven Copper‐Catalyzed Asymmetric Synthesis via Sequential C(sp 3 )─C(sp 3 ) Bond Scission
title: Copper catalysis for asymmetric cleavage of adjacent C–C bonds
blurb: Visible light drives a chiral bisoxazoline copper complex to cleave adjacent C(sp3)–C(sp3) bonds with control over chemoselectivity, regioselectivity, and stereochemistry, a long-standing synthetic challenge. Full substrate scope and how this compares to existing fragmentation methods remain to be seen.

original_title: Zymeworks Inc outlines its antibody-drug conjugate strategy as investors watch oncology partnerships - ad-hoc-news.de
title: Zymeworks outlines ADC strategy and partnerships
blurb: The company is laying out its approach to antibody-drug conjugates while framing several oncology partnerships in view. Whether that actually differentiates them from the other players crowding into ADCs is the open question.

original_title: Cu(II)-Catalyzed [3 + 1 + 1] Cascade Annulation of Sulfoxonium Ylides with 2-Naphthols and N , N -Dimethylaminoethanol as a C1 Synthon: Direct Access to Naphtho[2,1- b ]furans
title: Building naphtho[2,1-b]furans via Cu-catalyzed cascade with sulfoxonium ylides
blurb: A [3+1+1] annulation using Cu(II) to couple sulfoxonium ylides with naphthols and a one-carbon source. The cascade logic is clean but narrow substrate windows are the typical tax on these highly optimized reactions.

original_title: Advanced Peptide Modifications for Superior Therapeutics
title: Peptide modifications for drug applications
blurb: A Biochemistry review on tuning peptides for better therapeutic performance. Without the full article the specifics are hard to gauge, but peptide engineering is active enough that something here probably matters.

original_title: A radical–polar crossover approach to complex nitrogen heterocycles via the triplet state
title: Building nitrogen heterocycles via radical-polar crossover in the triplet state
blurb: A synthetic strategy that harnesses the triplet state to enable radical-polar crossover chemistry in constructing nitrogen rings. The mechanism is mechanistically clever but this class of tactic usually demands very specific substrates to run clean.

original_title: C–H Functionalization of Indoles and Naphthols with Dithiocarbamoyl Succinimides (DTCS): Synthesis of Indole-Dithiocarbamates and Naphthol-Carbamo(dithioperoxo)thioates
title: C–H coupling to indoles and naphthols via dithiocarbamoyl succinimides
blurb: Dithiocarbamoyl succinimides enabling C–H functionalization on aromatic indoles and naphthols to install sulfur-containing groups. DTCS reagents are still boutique and the scope is probably confined to electron-rich aromatics.

original_title: Photoredox/Copper-Catalyzed Enantioselective S-Alkylation of Sulfenamides with Hydrocarbons Enabled by Commercial BOX Ligands
title: Enantioselective S-alkylation of sulfenamides via photoredox and copper
blurb: BOX ligands steering an asymmetric S-alkylation of sulfenamides under photoredox-copper catalysis using hydrocarbons as the alkyl source. Photoredox-copper tactics are often narrow in substrate scope, so whether this generalizes beyond the BOX scaffold is the real test.

original_title: Desilylative Ring-Opening Strategy for Skipped 1,5-Diene Synthesis Under Synergistic Photoredox/Nickel Catalysis
title: Opening aryl cyclopropanes to skipped dienes via photoredox and nickel
blurb: Synergistic photoredox-nickel catalysis cracking aryl cyclopropanes into skipped 1,5-dienes via a benzylic radical stabilized through the β-silicon effect. The mechanism moves beyond conventional nucleophilic activation routes, though practical scope limits still need to be worked out.

original_title: Semiartificial CO 2 Fixation Using Metal-Dependent Formate Dehydrogenase
title: Enzyme-based CO2 reduction via formate dehydrogenase
blurb: A Chemical Reviews piece on hybrid systems pairing synthetic catalysis with formate dehydrogenase for CO2 fixation. Enzymes bring real selectivity advantages but getting them to tolerate non-native reaction conditions is still where most of these systems stumble.

original_title: Atomic‐Scale Charge Channelling in Poly(triazine imide) With Cooperative Ti–Ru Sites for Efficient Visible‐Light CO 2 Reduction
title: Atomic-scale charge routing in a Ti-Ru photocatalyst for CO2 reduction
blurb: A poly(triazine imide) photocatalyst with cooperative Ti and Ru sites engineered for atomic-scale charge direction and synchronized redox kinetics in visible-light CO2 reduction with water. The catalyst design is thoughtful, though photocatalytic CO2 work still struggles to move lab efficiency into practical rates.

original_title: Synthesis of Six Natural Products Enabled by Selective Hydroxylation of Orcinaldehyde‐Like Substrates With an Enzyme Library
title: Enzyme-guided C–H hydroxylation for rapid access to natural products
blurb: An enzyme library enabling site-selective hydroxylation of orcinaldehyde-type substrates as a streamlined route to six natural products. The selectivity is real when the enzymes align with the substrate, but expanding this to new scaffolds requires fresh enzyme engineering each time.

original_title: A Ni-Mediated Cross-Coupling Approach to Deuterated 18 F- Fluoromethylated (Hetero)arenes
title: Nickel coupling simplifies 18F fluoromethylation precursors
blurb: A Ni-mediated route to 18F,D2-fluoromethylated (hetero)arenes improves on SNAr radiochemistry by using more stable precursors and avoiding bespoke syntheses. It's a real convenience for PET labeling, though narrower in scope than the established methods.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Cobalt-Stabilized Propargylic Oxocarbenium Ions Enable Direct and Asymmetric Nickel(II) Catalyzed Aldol-Like Reactions
title: Aldol couplings with aliphatic acetals finally work
blurb: Cobalt-stabilized propargylic acetals paired with a chiral nickel(II) catalyst handle asymmetric aldol-like couplings that normally fail. It addresses a real limitation of aliphatic acetals, though the substrate set is tight.

original_title: Multiatom Skeletal Editing to Access SeCN-Containing Bicyclo[1.1.1]pentanes with Antitumor Activity
title: SeCN-containing bicyclo[1.1.1]pentanes with antitumor activity
blurb: Multiatom skeletal editing produces bicyclic scaffolds bearing selenium and cyanide that show antitumor activity. It's a new scaffold worth tracking, though preliminary activity is the story here.

original_title: CXCR4-targeted PET imaging of glioblastoma using [68Ga]Ga-TD-01: from pharmacokinetics and dosimetry to theranostic potential
title: A 68Ga PET tracer for CXCR4 imaging in glioblastoma
blurb: [68Ga]Ga-TD-01 targets CXCR4, a receptor involved in glioblastoma invasion and therapy resistance, for PET imaging and potential theranostics. The target is mechanistically sound, but GBM imaging has a track record of preclinical promise that doesn't always translate clinically.

original_title: TiO 2 -Catalyzed Borylation of Haloarenes under Visible Light and Synthesis of Benzoxaboroles
title: Visible-light borylation of haloarenes with TiO2
blurb: A TiO2 photocatalyst borylates haloarenes under visible light and enables benzoxaborole synthesis. It's a straightforward extension of photocatalytic C–B coupling, useful if the scope is solid.

original_title: Emulsion droplet-enabled selective electrochemical oxidation of alcohols at industrially relevant current densities
title: Emulsion droplets enable TEMPO oxidation at industrial currents
blurb: Emulsion microenvironments let TEMPO-mediated electrochemical oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes reach industrially relevant current densities with high selectivity and broad substrate scope. TEMPO chemistry isn't new, but electrochemical scaling at these currents is genuinely noteworthy.

original_title: Green Analytical Chemistry Approach In RP-HPLC For Quantitative Estimations Of Tyrosine Kinase Inhibitor
title: A green HPLC method for pexidartinib quantification
blurb: A validated, green RP-HPLC method quantifies pexidartinib (a CSF-1R inhibitor) in drug formulations with stability-indicating capability. It's solid analytical chemistry, though green HPLC advances typically lack generalizability beyond the specific drug.
venue: Zenodo (CERN European Organization for Nuclear Research)

original_title: Front Cover: Monosubstituted N ‐Arylhydroxylamine Chemistry: Integrating Contemporary Synthetic Approaches for the Efficient Construction of Diverse Heterocyclic Scaffolds
title: N-arylhydroxylamine chemistry for heterocyclic scaffolds
blurb: Monosubstituted N-arylhydroxylamines provide efficient access to diverse heterocyclic scaffolds through contemporary synthetic approaches. It's useful methodology for heterocycle synthesis, assuming the methods offer real advantages over established routes.

original_title: Electrosynthesis of Glycine From Nitrate and Glyoxylic Acid Over a Bi 2 S 3 Nanosheets‐Based Catalyst
title: Electrocatalytic glycine synthesis from nitrate and glyoxylic acid
blurb: Bi2S3 nanosheets catalyze electrochemical C–N coupling of nitrate and glyoxylic acid to glycine under mild conditions with renewable electricity. It's a clever feedstock combination, though selectivity and whether this scales beyond the lab are still open questions.

original_title: Exploring synthetic routes to 6-functionalized 4-azaspiro[2.3]hexanes
title: Routes to 6-functionalized 4-azaspiro[2.3]hexanes
blurb: These 4-azaspiro[2.3]hexanes serve as piperidine bioisosteres in medicinal chemistry, with methods to add substituents at the 6-position. The scaffold is plausible, though whether it outperforms standard piperidines in drug discovery depends on conformational properties and SAR.

original_title: Enantioselective Construction of Inherently Chiral Saddle-Shaped Azepines via Palladium-Catalyzed Intramolecular C–H Arylation
title: Saddle-shaped azepines with controlled stereochemistry
blurb: Intramolecular C–H arylation under palladium catalysis to construct chiral azepine rings bearing inherent saddle-shaped geometry in enantioenriched form. It's solid synthetic work, though whether the saddle shape itself matters is application-dependent.

original_title: Base-Catalyzed 1,6-Conjugate Addition/ ipso -Cyclization of Ynone-Tethered Pyrroles with para -Quinone Methides: Access to Spiro-Fused Cyclohexadienone/Pyrrolizine Frameworks
title: Spiro pyrrolizines from a base-catalyzed cascade
blurb: Ynone-tethered pyrroles condense with para-quinone methides in a single base-catalyzed pot, combining 1,6-conjugate addition and ipso-cyclization to make spiro-fused cyclohexadienone/pyrrolizine structures. One-pot cascades are nice when they deliver the regiochemistry and stereochemistry cleanly.

original_title: Current status of radionuclide therapy targeting PARP in cancer: challenges and prospects
title: Radiolabeled PARP inhibitors for targeted cancer therapy
blurb: A review surveying PARP inhibitors labeled with various radionuclides for targeted cancer therapy, focusing on isotope selection and radiobiological properties. PARP is a validated target, so the question is which isotope and delivery strategy actually work in clinical settings.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging
